entries.current_owner: Swift application and widget shells
entries.target_owner: Swift and future Kotlin platform shells
entries.reason: Composition, native navigation, intents, widgets, and transient presentation are platform responsibilities.
entries.boundary: Dispatch typed application intents and render bounded projections.
entries.persisted_state: Widget app-group snapshot only; never an authoritative domain store.

entries.current_owner: Swift AVFoundation and MediaPlayer adapters
entries.target_owner: Swift AVFoundation and Kotlin Media3 adapters
entries.reason: Audio sessions, routes, media controls, and composition execute OS capabilities.
entries.boundary: Execute typed playback/audio requests and return bounded raw observations.
entries.persisted_state: None; durable playback policy belongs to the shared core.

entries.current_owner: Swift generated-core integration, typed host dispatch, and bounded platform capabilities
entries.target_owner: Swift and future Kotlin projection and platform-capability adapters
entries.reason: Native shells translate bounded projections into platform-observable presentation state and execute typed platform primitives requested by the shared core.
entries.boundary: Use generated facade types directly; do not mirror durable models or make product decisions in adapters. Host capabilities return correlated typed observations without durable writes.
entries.persisted_state: None.

entries.current_owner: Swift one-shot legacy workflow reader, exact retirement transaction, and read-only rollback manifests
entries.target_owner: Deleted after the documented post-#111 rollback support window closes
entries.reason: The Rust workflow cutover must safely classify and preserve legacy Swift job rows before Rust becomes authoritative, but this compatibility path is not product behavior and must not become a second source of truth.
entries.boundary: Decode legacy model and publisher rows only through a quarantined compatibility type. Stage reconstructable model evidence in Rust, durably publish and verify immutable full-row manifests, compare-delete exact rows, and atomically commit the final Swift-retirement marker before any native coordinator starts. After the marker, the path only verifies absence and reads rollback evidence.
entries.persisted_state: Versioned integrity-checked model and publisher manifests plus a versioned retirement marker. Rust remains the only authoritative workflow writer after cutover; no restore API exists.
entries.deletion_target: Issue #114 removes the compatibility decoder and retirement command after at least two production releases and 90 days, once supported installations are proven across the marker; immutable export support may remain read-only.

entries.current_owner: Swift one-shot scheduled-agent source reader, immutable rollback manifest, and exact retirement transaction
entries.target_owner: Deleted after the issue #130 rollback support window closes
entries.reason: Existing Swift definitions, jobs, artifacts, and completed conversation output must be preserved and classified before Rust becomes the only scheduled-agent writer.
entries.boundary: Read and back up the exact legacy source, map it to typed cutover inputs, retire exact matching Swift rows before Rust authority commits, and never expose a restore writer.
entries.persisted_state: Versioned integrity-checked rollback manifests only; Rust owns all active scheduled definitions, occurrences, attempts, recurrence, and generated artifacts after cutover.
entries.deletion_target: Remove the compatibility mapper, source readers, and retirement command after supported installations are proven across the scheduled-agent cutover marker; immutable export evidence may remain read-only.

entries.current_owner: Swift decode-only chat/activity sources, immutable rollback manifest, typed mapper, exact source retirement, and exact removal of retired native diagnostic duplicates
entries.target_owner: Deleted after the issue #138 rollback support window closes
entries.reason: Existing Swift conversations must survive the transition into Rust without preserving a native writer or long-lived private activity and diagnostic stores.
entries.boundary: Decode and back up the exact legacy conversation file, map supported conversations to typed Rust inputs, delete the exact matching chat source before Rust authority commits, then remove the retired AgentRunLog/runs.json and embedded activity duplicates after conversation and memory authority are verified. Expose no restore or mutation API.
entries.persisted_state: Versioned integrity-checked conversation rollback manifests only; Rust owns all active and imported conversation history. The embedded activity payload is decode-only until its verified one-shot retirement.
entries.deletion_target: Remove the legacy conversation decoder, backup, mapper, and cutover adapter after supported installations are proven across the Rust authority marker; immutable rollback evidence may remain read-only.

entries.current_owner: Swift one-shot memory metadata reader, immutable rollback manifest, and exact source retirement
entries.target_owner: Deleted after the issue #138 rollback support window closes
entries.reason: Existing native memory and compiled-memory records must survive the transition without retaining Swift as a durable writer.
entries.boundary: Back up and map the exact legacy snapshot into typed Rust inputs, retire exact matching metadata before Rust authority commits, and expose no restore or post-cutover native mutation path.
entries.persisted_state: Versioned integrity-checked rollback manifests only; Rust owns active memories, revisions, compiled provenance, and all post-cutover commands.
entries.deletion_target: Remove the backup, cutover, and retirement adapters after supported installations are proven across the Rust memory authority marker; immutable rollback evidence may remain read-only.

entries.current_owner: Swift URLSession and platform credential capability adapters
entries.target_owner: Swift and future Kotlin primitive provider executors
entries.reason: HTTP execution, streamed byte bounds, and secure platform credential access are native capabilities; semantic qualification and durable policy remain in Rust.
entries.boundary: Execute the exact Rust-planned typed request and return bounded provider envelopes, metadata, and raw typed failures without prompt construction, provider selection, semantic parsing, retry, fallback, or persistence.
entries.persisted_state: None; credentials remain in platform security facilities and the adapters retain no response state.

entries.current_owner: Swift transient agent-composition task shell and raw observation mapping
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust agent workflow/application layer issuing typed native host requests
entries.reason: Issue #110 deleted the model branch after its Rust workflow activated; only agent-composed chapter observations remain temporarily outside a Rust-issued host request.
entries.boundary: Map a bounded agent composition into a typed observation, call Rust qualification, and retain only bounded in-memory task identities.
entries.persisted_state: None; no files, database rows, retry counters, selected artifacts, or canonical mirrors.
entries.deletion_target: Issue #111 replaces the remaining agent composition shell with a Rust-issued agent workflow request; primitive platform capabilities remain native.

entries.current_owner: Swift bounded chapter projection and active-view adapters
entries.target_owner: Swift and future Kotlin native projection adapters
entries.reason: Native player and episode-detail surfaces render platform models reconstructed from bounded Rust pages without owning durable chapter facts.
entries.boundary: Retain at most eight active episode scopes, page Rust projections with a fixed cap, and clear transient values when the final native scope closes.
entries.persisted_state: None; Episode chapter and ad fields are decode-only legacy inputs and replaceable active-view projections.

entries.current_owner: Swift provider observation mapper and bounded Rust projection adapter
entries.target_owner: Swift and future Kotlin native capability/projection adapters
entries.reason: Native providers produce platform values and native presentation consumes transcript pages, while Rust owns canonical artifacts, selection, and readiness.
entries.boundary: Map native observations once into generated commands and reconstruct presentation values only from bounded Rust projections.
entries.persisted_state: None; the adapter owns no durable transcript copy or selection policy.

entries.current_owner: Swift design and Apple speech/audio capture
entries.target_owner: Platform-native presentation and capture adapters
entries.reason: Typography, haptics, accessibility, animation, and platform speech/capture remain native.
entries.boundary: Return raw capture/transcription observations; do not decide durable workflow policy.
entries.persisted_state: Transient buffers and OS handles only.

entries.current_owner: Swift local product-signal adapter
entries.target_owner: Platform-native privacy and evaluation adapters
entries.reason: Content-free evaluation observations, local consent controls, and manual OS sharing are native product-validation infrastructure rather than authoritative domain state.
entries.boundary: Accept typed committed outcomes from domain owners; never influence commands, projections, or durable product decisions.
entries.persisted_state: Bounded local signal archive with a random, user-rotatable install identifier.

entries.current_owner: Rust owns migrated listening, note, clip, memory, and selected canonical transcript records; Swift retains presentation mappings, read-only legacy import values, and unmigrated Codable domains
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust domain crate
entries.reason: Durable identities, settings, notes, clips, memory, and agent facts are cross-platform product state.
entries.boundary: Stable IDs, versioned schemas, commands, and bounded projections.
entries.persisted_state: Rust SQLite for migrated listening, notes, clips, and transcripts; AppState metadata for unmigrated domains only.
entries.deletion_target: Remove migrated Swift records and Codable ownership after each vertical-slice cutover.

entries.current_owner: Rust listening domain with Swift presentation and read-only legacy import values
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust listening domain
entries.reason: Podcast, episode, subscription, feed normalization, and policy are stable cross-platform behavior.
entries.boundary: Native fetch returns bytes/validators; Rust normalizes feeds and owns library projections.
entries.persisted_state: Rust SQLite listening tables; Swift values are replaceable projections and legacy import compatibility.
entries.deletion_target: Delete Swift feed/domain ownership while retaining thin platform fetch/export adapters.

entries.current_owner: Rust is the listening, notes, and clips writer; Swift AppStateStore persists unmigrated domains and applies native projections
entries.target_owner: Rust application actor and versioned Rust SQLite schemas
entries.reason: Durable application state and mutation policy require one cross-platform writer.
entries.boundary: Swift renders projections and dispatches commands; importer reads legacy Swift state once.
entries.persisted_state: Rust listening/note/clip tables plus AppState metadata for unmigrated domains; migrated projections are stripped from Swift persistence.
entries.deletion_target: Delete migrated AppState fields/mutation methods; retain only supported read-only import and native projection adapters.

entries.current_owner: Rust owns publisher and model chapter workflow intent/lifecycle; Swift still owns unmigrated workflow kinds, job rows, and artifact receipts
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust workflow/application layer
entries.reason: Desired state, leases, fencing, retry, recovery, and artifact adoption are durable cross-platform decisions.
entries.boundary: Native executes BGTask, URLSession, notification, and platform provider capabilities.
entries.persisted_state: Rust publisher/model chapter workflow rows plus versioned Swift workflow/artifact tables and staged files for unmigrated kinds; no chapter workflow row remains in Swift.
entries.deletion_target: Issue #119 deleted Swift download admission, scheduling, reconciliation, and durable artifact ownership. Issue #130 deletes scheduled-agent planning, execution, recovery, artifact completion, and recurrence ownership immediately after its staged Rust cutover.

entries.current_owner: Swift bounded artifact verification executor
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust workflow and artifact application layer
entries.reason: Issue #48 removes visible main-actor stalls without delaying product proof; the typed request/result boundary is explicitly temporary until the workflow slice migrates.
entries.boundary: Native callers submit typed file evidence requests; only verified typed results may reach fenced workflow commits.
entries.persisted_state: None; exact immutable file identities are cached in memory only.
entries.deletion_target: Issue #119 deletes download verification use after Rust adoption; #60 deletes the executor when the final unmigrated artifact workflow no longer calls it.

entries.current_owner: Rust canonical transcript and chapter/ad artifacts, selections, provenance, migration state, and playback policy; Swift retains bounded native projections and raw capabilities
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust transcript/knowledge domain
entries.reason: Normalized segments, speakers, timestamps, chapters, and provenance are durable evidence.
entries.boundary: Provider/native capabilities return raw typed payloads; Rust normalizes and versions artifacts.
entries.persisted_state: Rust SQLite owns canonical transcript and chapter/ad immutable history, selections, command receipts, and authority state. Legacy Swift values are decode-only migration evidence.
entries.deletion_target: Completed by #110/#112: Swift chapter prompt, model-selection, mode-selection, input-version policy, durable workflow writer, and obsolete model adapter path were deleted. Retain only primitive provider execution and bounded native presentation adapters.

entries.current_owner: Swift raw provider HTTP capability executors; Rust owns provider choice, request identity, polling, retry, cancellation, and recovery policy
entries.target_owner: Swift and Kotlin primitive provider capability adapters behind Rust workflows
entries.reason: Credential custody and raw network execution are native capabilities; provider workflow decisions and durable evidence are cross-platform Rust policy.
entries.boundary: Execute one typed provider action or status request and return bounded raw evidence; never poll, retry, select a provider, or persist workflow state.
entries.persisted_state: None in Swift; Rust stores external provider IDs, fences, retry timing, and recovery evidence.
entries.deletion_target: Delete any remaining Swift provider orchestration as its workflow kind cuts over; retain only raw typed HTTP capability execution.

entries.current_owner: Swift Speech framework adapter
entries.target_owner: Platform speech capability adapters
entries.reason: Apple Speech is an OS capability; Android will use its own native equivalent.
entries.boundary: Execute a typed request and return raw timed speech observations/failure.
entries.persisted_state: None in the adapter.

entries.current_owner: Swift typed provider status executor
entries.target_owner: Swift and Kotlin platform capability adapters
entries.reason: Credential custody and bounded provider HTTP execution remain native capabilities while Rust owns provider choice, workflow state, retry, and recovery timing.
entries.boundary: Execute exactly one authorized typed status request and return raw bounded status or transcript evidence; never poll, retry, fall back, or persist workflow state.
entries.persisted_state: None; external operation identity and recovery state remain in Rust.

entries.current_owner: Swift utility-model request and provider error mapping
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust workflow and agent application layers
entries.reason: Utility prompt execution, provider choice, and retry/error semantics drive durable cross-platform workflows.
entries.boundary: A future native adapter may execute a typed provider request, but Rust owns the request policy and resulting workflow state.
entries.persisted_state: None in the client; callers currently commit generated workflow artifacts.
entries.deletion_target: Delete Swift utility request policy when each durable workflow kind moves to the shared core.

entries.current_owner: Pod0 Rust recall-index crate and application facade
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust knowledge/application storage behind the typed facade
entries.reason: ADR-0006 selected Rust ownership; issue #106 cut schema, embedding cache, rebuild, scoped retrieval, recovery, limits, and cancellation over without dual writes.
entries.boundary: Rust consumes stable evidence and typed embedding observations, then returns only Rust-ranked recall projections; native code never owns raw lane policy or index state.
entries.persisted_state: A versioned disposable Rust sqlite-vec/FTS index plus reconstructible cached embedding observations, rebuilt exclusively from canonical Rust evidence.
entries.deletion_target: Completed by #106: Swift VectorIndex execution, its schema/query files, raw-candidate FFI variants, and RecallCapabilityService were deleted. The non-recall CSQLiteVec package bridge retained by legacy state/workflow stores is explicitly tracked by #107.

entries.current_owner: Pod0 Rust domain, storage, recall index, and application facade
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust application kernel
entries.reason: Provider/model choice, embedding-space identity, and reranker enablement are durable cross-platform product decisions. Issue #108 moved them behind the typed facade and removed Swift authority.
entries.boundary: Rust emits the exact typed provider, model, and dimensionality on each bounded host request. Native executes that request once using platform credential facilities and reports a typed result.
entries.persisted_state: Schema-versioned singleton configuration in the shared Rust store; provider credentials remain native and are never persisted by Rust.
entries.deletion_target: Completed by #108: ProviderEmbeddingsClient, Settings+Embeddings, active Swift recall settings, Swift reranker gating, and iCloud recall dual writes were deleted.

entries.current_owner: Swift URLSession, credential, embedding, reranking, and legacy-index file capability adapters
entries.target_owner: Swift and future Kotlin primitive recall-capability executors
entries.reason: Platform credentials, provider network execution, and sandbox file primitives remain native while Rust owns when they run, batching, cache identity, index state, ranking, retries, cutover state, and workflow outcomes.
entries.boundary: Execute one bounded typed provider or exact-file request and return an ordered or correlated observation without durable product decisions or writes to authoritative state.
entries.persisted_state: None; provider credentials remain in platform security facilities.

entries.current_owner: Rust owns interactive and scheduled turn state, conversation history, model usage, generated audio provenance, tool identity, permissions, fences, supported commits, and publication receipts; Swift retains typed provider/platform capabilities, transient presentation streams, and decode-only rollback compatibility
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust agent/application layer
entries.reason: Tool validation, permission, durable commits, jobs, and generated artifact policy must be identical across platforms.
entries.boundary: ADR-0008 and agent-tool-permissions.json require typed Rust proposals, durable grants, effect fences, commits, and bounded projections. Native presents conversations/approvals and returns raw observations for typed platform capabilities. pod0-nmp alone owns generic NMP engine, signing, routing, and receipt facts; the app facade accepts no relay selection.
entries.persisted_state: Rust SQLite owns conversations, turns, model usage, memories, scheduled tasks/occurrences, generated audio and artifact provenance, and publication receipts. Swift retains platform files awaiting acknowledged adoption and immutable rollback manifests only.
entries.deletion_target: Issues #130, #135, and #136 completed scheduled/conversation/artifact/publication authority cutovers. Facade v45 deleted native catalog and provider-neutral schema ownership. Issue #133 completed the product-proof Rust agent owner; #134 finishes native capability qualification, #137 qualifies the physical-device signer, and #138 removes remaining compatibility ownership.

entries.current_owner: Rust owns playback, chapter navigation, ad-skip, and interactive agent turn decisions; Swift dispatches typed controls and executes provider capabilities
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust listening and agent application domains
entries.reason: Queue/resume/completion and agent turn/tool decisions are durable cross-platform behavior.
entries.boundary: Swift feature views render projections; AVFoundation and LLM/provider capabilities report raw results.
entries.persisted_state: Rust owns playback resume/played state, selected canonical chapter artifacts, chapter sessions, skipped-ad state, and active interactive agent conversations and turns; legacy Swift records are decode-only rollback evidence.
entries.deletion_target: Scheduled and interactive agent controller, unsupported-tool policy, native catalog selection, and provider-neutral schema definitions have been deleted. Issue #138 removes the remaining legacy archive after migration while retaining thin native view/capability encoders.

entries.current_owner: Swift product-proof controllers
entries.target_owner: Shared knowledge/workflow policy after interaction validation
entries.reason: Auto-snip and recall answer presentation are still being validated behind typed feature/domain boundaries.
entries.boundary: Commit only through typed store/library interfaces; no new independent durable store.
entries.persisted_state: Clip records commit through the Rust facade; controller state is transient.
entries.deletion_target: Replace transcript-selection and workflow policy as those slices migrate; retain native capture UI and typed clip commands.

entries.current_owner: SwiftUI features and native presentation adapters
entries.target_owner: SwiftUI and future Compose presentation
entries.reason: Views, navigation, formatting, accessibility, settings presentation, capture UI, and transient view models stay native.
entries.boundary: Read projections and dispatch typed intents; do not open durable stores directly.
entries.persisted_state: Transient presentation only; current exceptions are tracked by shared/temporary entries.

entries.current_owner: Swift platform and security services
entries.target_owner: Platform-specific capability adapters
entries.reason: Credential custody, deep links, notifications, Spotlight, native export, widget snapshots, and URLSession delegates execute Apple capabilities.
entries.boundary: Accept typed requests and report raw results; no durable product-policy decisions.
entries.persisted_state: Keychain, iCloud KVS, app-group widget snapshot, and OS-managed transfer handles.

entries.current_owner: Swift owns explicitly temporary categorization, clip-boundary assistance, and remaining provider/workflow adapters
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust domain/application/workflow layers plus thin native capabilities
entries.reason: The residual mixed service layer contains unsettled product behavior and thin platform executors. Listening, subscription durability, downloads, transcript workflow state, scheduled agents, active conversations, generated audio, and Nostr receipt authority have already moved to Rust and must not reappear here.
entries.boundary: Migrated domains use typed Rust commands and projections. Temporary behavior writes only through its declared typed owner and may not create a second durable store.
entries.persisted_state: Temporary durable Swift state is limited to categories and explicitly unmigrated adjuncts. Migrated library, workflow, conversation, memory, artifact, activity, and publication facts remain Rust-owned or retired.
entries.deletion_target: Issues #119 and #130 reduced download and scheduled work to typed native capabilities. Issues #60, #133, #134, and #138 migrate or delete each residual temporary policy while retaining capabilities that are native by design.
